source_record_details.food_name: Bread baked after inoculation but not sterile
source_record_details.conditions: Inoculation in/on previously heated (cooked, baked, pasteurized, etc) but not sterilised food/medium
source_record_details.citation: Food Standards Agency funded data generated at the former FMBRA, Chorleywood (today Champden and Chorleywood Food Research Association), UK

construction_notes: elicited_inputs derived from ComBase record_id 8994 (Bread / Bread baked after inoculation but not sterile). N0 = log_cfu at lowest step. past/future_temperature_segments are an approximate human-readable split of the curve's duration (constant temperature); ground_truth_curve.values is the actual gridded trajectory. Some grid steps may be linearly interpolated between real lab readings rather than directly measured -- this is expected given sparse irregular sampling gridded onto fixed 15.15h intervals, not a data quality issue. Not a generalization claim -- used to sanity-check the elicit -> infer -> verdict pipeline end to end.
